title: Memory Representation of a 2D Array
description: A visual comparison of Row-Major and Column-Major order for storing a 2D array in linear computer memory, showing the sequence of memory addresses.
elements: Row-Major Order Path (a line traversing the grid row-by-row)
Column-Major Order Path (a line traversing the grid column-by-column)
relationships: Row-Major Order stores elements of row 0 first, followed by row 1
Column-Major Order stores elements of column 0 first, followed by column 1, then column 2
Both mappings transform the 2D logical indices into a 1D physical address offset
